danger_signals.visual_form: River of lava in Scorpion's Lair stage; bright orange/red flowing hazard
danger_signals.meaning: Stage Fatality hazard—uppercut opponent into lava for instant kill; environmental danger zone

danger_signals.visual_form: Kahn's Kave background with shadowed rock formations
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile territory associated with villain Shao Kahn; danger imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Scorpion's Lair stage visual design combines scorpion imagery with infernal atmosphere
danger_signals.meaning: Scorpion is a vengeful anti-hero; stage represents his dangerous nature and supernatural origin

danger_signals.visual_form: Jade's Desert shows Cyrax stuck waist-deep in sand; barren landscape
danger_signals.meaning: Quicksand trap—danger of being trapped or consumed by environment

danger_signals.visual_form: Blue Portal/Lost stage combines portal imagery with Pit bridge and mountains
danger_signals.meaning: Mystical danger; liminal space where fighters are displaced or disoriented

safety_signals.visual_form: Standard fighting stage layouts with defined boundaries
safety_signals.meaning: Organized, rule-bound combat space—danger is contained within competitive framework
Open, neutral territory; less enclosed than cavern stages

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Scorpion's Lair stage is his domain; represents his vengeful nature
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Lava rivers, fiery underworld setting, dark rock formations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Kahn's Kave stage represents his territory and power

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character-specific visual designs; ninja aesthetics for several
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each character has stage associated with their origin or story
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Jade's Desert stage references Jade's MK3 ending and story arc

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Returning characters (Reptile, Kitana, Jade, Scorpion) have dedicated stages and biographies in attract mode; visual presence reasserts their narrative importance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: These four characters receive special story treatment with biographies shown during attract mode; their narrative arcs are privileged over MK3 roster characters whose full-body portraits and detailed biographies are removed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Eight-player Tournament mode and 2-on-2 Endurance match modes visualized through arena progression; Shao Kahn's Lost Treasures appear after completion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Winning triggers cinematic rewards and story progression; masters over higher difficulty reflect escalating threat level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: New backgrounds cycle—Scorpion's Lair, Jade's Desert, River Kombat, Kahn's Kave, Blue Portal; original MK3 stages only appear after UMK3 backgrounds cycle twice
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stage Fatalities introduce new environmental kill methods (lava uppercut in Scorpion's Lair); quicksand trap threat in Jade's Desert shows environmental storytelling of Cyrax's fate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Computer opponent's predictable behaviors (always throws projectile when backflipped into, mimics player walking, fails to stumble when cornered) visually represent AI weakness
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players exploit these patterns; victory becomes about recognizing opponent behavioral tells rather than pure combat skill—tells story of exploitable weaknesses in system

environmental_storytelling.example: Scorpion's Lair/Hell stage directly visualizes Scorpion's supernatural origin and vengeful nature through infernal lava environment; his story is told through where he fights
In Jade's Desert, Cyrax is visually stuck waist-deep in sand in the background—tells his story from MK3 ending without dialogue; visual reference to previous game's narrative
Blue Portal/Lost stage combines visual elements from multiple locations (Pit 3 bridge, Pit II mountains, Choose Your Destiny screen)—suggests displacement, convergence of timelines or realities
UMK3 stages must cycle twice before original MK3 stages appear; visual/mechanical progression mirrors narrative escalation toward confrontation with Shao Kahn

Removal of full biographies and character portraits for MK3 roster (only Kitana, Jade, Scorpion, Reptile shown in attract mode) visually de-prioritizes them; suggests narrative focus shift to returning characters
Stage Fatality mechanics (lava uppercut in Scorpion's Lair) make environment a third combatant; stages actively participate in determining outcomes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Always throws projectile during jump kick after backflip—predictable, exploitable pattern telegraphs vulnerability

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Proximity to player within certain range
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Mimics player's back-and-forth walking movements; ceases attacking entirely—behavioral synchronization indicates AI loss of autonomous decision-making
Fails to stumble or retreat from repeated punches; remains static—visual sign of system breaking down, player can win trivially
Presents Lost Treasures (selectable rewards); voice line 'Outstanding' triggers if Smoke code entered—recognition of player achievement; shows Kahn as reward-giver/final authority
Featured in attract mode biographies with visual prominence; positioned as special/restored to roster—behavioral tell of their story significance despite removal from MK3

notes: UMK3 SNES operates within fighting game paradigm where visual world-state communication is subordinate to mechanical/competitive readability. Danger signals primarily manifest through stage aesthetics (lava, darkness, environmental hazards) rather than dynamic world-state shifts. Faction identity is character-centric rather than geopolitical; stages serve as character extensions. Political situation (if any) is implicit in Shao Kahn's tournament structure and the narrative of character restoration/reintegration post-MK3. The game's real manifestation layer operates through: (1) Stage cycling order (narrative progression), (2) Environmental hazards as interactive story elements, (3) Missing content (character portraits/biographies) as negative space narrative, (4) Backgrounded NPC states (Cyrax in sand) as visual exposition. AI behavioral tells are technical artifacts that paradoxically communicate weakness/vulnerability rather than danger.
